title: From Recommendations to Actions: A Comparison of OFE’s submission of 3 February 2026 with Section 4 of COM(2026) 503 final (adopted 3 June 2026)
content:
The European Commission’s European Technological Sovereignty Package includes a full strategy for EU open digital ecosystems. OpenForum Europe submitted a contribution to the European Commission’s call for evidence on 3 February 2026 and the Commission published a comprehensive package on 3 June 2026.

The headline outcome is clear: Open Source now has a standalone EU strategy inside the technological sovereignty package, sitting next to the Chips Act 2.0 and CADA proposals. That elevation is the clearest win for OFE’s argument that Open Source should be treated as a strategic priority.  

We already provided a first reaction on the package, as well as on the Strategy for EU Open Digital Ecosystems, now it is time for a deeper look into what OFE recommended and how those recommendations are reflected in the published strategy. 

Strong alignment: The strategy matches OFE on the points where OFE pushed hardest on instruments rather than framing.

  • demand-side and procurement action including an Open Source first principle and the public sector as anchor customer; 
  • a dedicated Open Source Maintenance Instrument; 
  • an ENISA-supported list of most exposed dependencies;

creator: Laszlo Igenczi
sourceDescription: Open Technologies for a Competitive and Sovereign Europe
